scheme_name: Financial Loan for Training of Commercial Pilot
page_title: Financial Loan for Training of Commercial Pilot

details: The "Financial Loan for Training of Commercial Pilot" scheme was launched by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Government of Gujarat. The scheme aims to support students from Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC) in obtaining Commercial Pilot License (CPL) training through financial assistance. Under this scheme, a loan is offered at a concessional interest rate to eligible students who meet the educational and institutional requirements for CPL training.
benefits: Up to ₹25,00,000 for Commercial Pilot License training.
2.5% additional interest for late or defaulted payments.
Principal: To be repaid within 10 years from the date of disbursement.
Interest: To be paid within 2 years, making the total repayment duration 12 years.
Loan recovery will begin one year after the loan is disbursed to the student.
eligibility: The applicant must belong to a Socially and Educationally Backward Class (SEBC).
The applicant must have passed Matriculation, Higher Secondary, Indian School Certificate, or an equivalent examination.
The applicant must meet all academic, technical, and other criteria as specified by the training institution.
The applicant must provide one solvent guarantor.
The applicant or their guardian must own property to mortgage in favor of the government.

online: The eligible applicant may visit the e-Samaj Kalyan Portal:
On the home page, under the tab ‘Citizen Login’, click on New User ‘
Enter your Full Name as per Aadhaar Card, Gender, Date of Birth, Aadhaar Card Number, Email ID, Caste etc. and then click on ‘Register’.
After successful registration, the applicants can login through their User ID and Password.
Now, click on ‘User Profile’ to update your profile.
Fill in all the mandatory information and then click on ‘Update’.
After updating the profile successfully, select the scheme appearing on the home page; an application form will open.
Fill in all the mandatory details in the application form and upload all the relevant documents.
Now agree with the terms & conditions and click on ‘Save Application’.

Submit the application form and take out the print of the application for future reference. The applicant can note down the application number to track the application status by logging into the portal using their User ID and Password.
Submit two physical copies of the form with documents to the concerned District Office.
The District Office verifies the application and forwards it to the Director, Office of Welfare of Developing Castes.
Incomplete applications are returned to the student for corrections.
Once approved, the guardian must mortgage their property with a registered deed in favor of the government.
After submitting the original registered mortgage deed, the loan amount is disbursed to the student’s bank account.

documents_required: Family Income Certificate, IT Return, Form-16
Copies of educational qualification certificates (from Class 10 to the last examination)
If training is abroad, a copy of the trainee's passport, a copy of the visa for that country, and a copy of the air ticket
Property documents and valuation report of the trainee's father/guardian
Guarantor: One solvent guarantor must be provided.

faqs.question: What is the maximum loan amount I can get under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The applicant can get a loan of up to ₹25,00,000/- at a concessional interest rate is 4% per annum.

faqs.question: Is there any penalty for late repayment?
faqs.answer: Yes, a penal interest of 2.5% is charged on defaults.

faqs.question: What is the duration for loan repayment?
faqs.answer: Principal must be repaid within 10 years and interest within 2 years.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Students from Socially and Educationally Backward Classes with required educational qualifications.

faqs.question: Is there any income limit to apply?
faqs.answer: No, there is no income limit.

faqs.question: Can the loan be used for pilot training abroad?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you have valid admission and documents for overseas training.

faqs.question: What documents are needed for applying?
faqs.answer: Caste certificate, education certificates, income proof, property documents, and admission letter, among others.

faqs.question: Do I need a guarantor?
faqs.answer: Yes, one solvent guarantor is mandatory.

faqs.question: Is mortgage of property necessary?
faqs.answer: Yes, either the guardian's or the guarantor's property must be mortgaged.

faqs.question: How do I apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Apply online at https://esamajkalyan.gujarat.gov.in, upload documents, and submit the form.

faqs.question: Can I apply offline?
faqs.answer: No, only online applications are accepted.

faqs.question: What if my application is incomplete?
faqs.answer: It will be returned to you for necessary corrections.

faqs.question: Who verifies and approves the loan?
faqs.answer: The District Office and the Director, Office of Welfare of Developing Castes.

faqs.question: How will the loan amount be disbursed?
faqs.answer: After mortgage registration, the amount is transferred directly to the student’s bank account.
